title: How to put £ sign before converted currency formula?
excerpt:
Hi There, 
I have a formula in one of my cells that converts dollars to pounds - 
  
However, I would like to put £ sign before the number in the formula. 
Any help would be jolly well appreciated! 
Thanks 
Leon

posts.author: leon.cliff (Leon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi There,

I have a formula in one of my cells that converts dollars to pounds -

image557×528 36.5 KB

However, I would like to put £ sign before the number in the formula.

Any help would be jolly well appreciated!

Thanks
Leon

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@leon.cliff

There are two primary ways of doing that with formula columns.

The first is to add a TEXT() function to the formula:

image725×494 30.6 KB

The second (usually preferred) is to set the format parameters of the column in the summary row. If you have turned off the summary for the column, you will need to turn it back on temporarily to set the parameters:

image824×361 20.8 KB

Jim - The Monday Man (YouTube Channel)

posts.author: leon.cliff (Leon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Jim, much obliged! I had no idea about the second, option. Absolutely brilliant stuff.

Have a good weekend and thanks again.

Leon
